entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The ability of soil to provide essential nutrients for plant growth.

entities.name: NUTRIENT RECOMMENDATIONS
entities.description: Guidelines for the application of nutrients based on soil tests.

entities.name: LAND USE PLANNING
entities.description: The process of regulating land use to achieve efficient resource management.

entities.name: GREEN REVOLUTION
entities.description: A period of agricultural transformation involving high-yielding varieties and improved technology.

entities.name: HIGH-YIELDING VARIETIES
entities.description: Crop varieties developed to produce higher yields.

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of growing crops for food or other purposes.

entities.name: SOIL DEGRADATION
entities.description: The decline in soil quality and productivity.

entities.name: CROP YIELDS
entities.description: The amount of agricultural production harvested per unit area.

entities.name: FERTILIZER
entities.description: Chemical or natural substances added to soil to increase its fertility.

entities.name: PLANT NUTRIENTS
entities.description: Essential chemical elements required by plants for growth.

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY MAPS
entities.description: Spatial representations of soil nutrient levels across a geographical area.

entities.name: STRATIFIED MULTISTAGE STRATIFIED RANDOM SAMPLING
entities.description: A sampling technique used to collect soil samples from different geographical locations.

entities.name: KRIGING
entities.description: A geostatistical interpolation method used for spatial prediction.

entities.name: AKAIKE'S INFORMATION CRITERION
entities.description: A method for model selection used to determine the appropriate kriging method.

entities.name: SOIL TEST VALUES
entities.description: Measured concentrations of nutrients in soil samples.
